pattern: Rotate coordinates by 45° (u=x+y, v=x-y) so Manhattan distance becomes Chebyshev distance (max of |du|, |dv|); the maximum pairwise distance then equals max(u)-min(u) or max(v)-min(v); try removing each candidate extremal point.
hints: Manhattan distance |x1-x2|+|y1-y2| = max(|(x1+y1)-(x2+y2)|, |(x1-y1)-(x2-y2)|) after the rotation u=x+y, v=x-y. So the maximum pairwise Manhattan distance equals max(max_u-min_u, max_v-min_v).
The point achieving the overall maximum must be one of the four extremal points (argmax u, argmin u, argmax v, argmin v). Try removing each of these at most 4 candidates and recompute the maximum.
For each candidate removal, compute the new max over u and v spans using the remaining points (use sorted arrays or track second extremes). Take the minimum over all 4 removal attempts.

edge_inputs.description: all three points identical, removing any gives distance 0
points forming a triangle, removing farthest shrinks span
removing the point that achieves max in u still leaves a large v span
two points are symmetric extremes; removing either gives same result

stress.description: random point sets of moderate size to verify coordinate rotation approach
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    points = [[rng.randint(1, 10**8), rng.randint(1, 10**8)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [points]
